title: Towards a theory of singular symplectic varieties
speaker: Aleksey Zinger
abstract: Singular algebraic (sub)varieties are fundamental to the theory of smooth projective manifolds. In parallel with his introduction of pseudo-holomorphic curve techniques into symplectic topology 30 years ago, Gromov asked about the feasibility of introducing notions of singular (sub)varieties suitable for this field. I will describe a new perspective on this question and motivate its appropriateness in the case of normal crossings singularities.

proof_stages.label: Program from algebraic geometry to symplectic topology
proof_stages.summary: The talk motivates a program, inspired by Gromov, Donaldson, mirror symmetry, and Gross-Siebert, to define symplectic analogues of singular varieties, subvarieties, and multifold symplectic constructions.

proof_stages.label: Normal-crossing local model
proof_stages.summary: The basic singularities to imitate are normal-crossing degenerations locally modeled by z1...zn=lambda, whose central fiber is a union of coordinate hyperplanes with strata given by their intersections.

proof_stages.label: GW-theoretic target and existing models
proof_stages.summary: The desired theory should define Gromov-Witten invariants of a normal-crossing central fiber and split them into relative invariants of its components, generalizing the known twofold symplectic sum and connecting with algebraic and exploded-geometric approaches.

proof_stages.label: Symplectic normal-crossing divisor definition
proof_stages.summary: A symplectic normal-crossing divisor is defined as a locally finite union of transverse smooth codimension-two branches for which every stratum is symplectic and its symplectic orientation agrees with the intersection orientation.

proof_stages.label: Regularizations and weak homotopy equivalence theorem
proof_stages.summary: The speaker introduces omega-regularizations and compatible almost-complex structures giving standard local models, then states that the projection from these structured triples to the purely symplectic space is a weak homotopy equivalence, which is enough for deformation-invariant constructions.

proof_stages.label: Canonical structures attached to symplectic divisors and varieties
proof_stages.summary: The regularization theorem yields symplectic notions of normal-crossing divisors, varieties, and degenerations, and lets one associate deformation-invariant objects such as the divisor line bundle, logarithmic tangent bundle, and normal bundle of the singular locus.

proof_stages.label: Smoothing criterion and n-fold symplectic cuts
proof_stages.summary: Applications include a smoothing/movability criterion for normal-crossing symplectic varieties in terms of the first Chern class of the singular-locus line bundle, and an n-fold symplectic cut construction from trace-zero torus actions generalizing Gompf and Lerman.

proof_stages.label: Proof strategy by local deformation and depth induction
proof_stages.summary: The proof of the regularization theorem deforms local symplectic forms into compatible standard forms while preserving closedness and nondegeneracy, then patches these deformations using an induction on the depth of the normal-crossing stratification.
